digest.greeting: Jean, today stayed centered on the EPMO update path, Amazon Connect, intake routing, and SMS Automation. The clearest completed movement I could ground from today’s mail is the RNWLS Missing EAD Inquiry being routed through CM-CoE, while the dashboard checklist keeps the main project follow-ups open for tomorrow.

items.label: Routed the RNWLS Missing EAD Inquiry through CM-CoE
items.detail: Jean sent the request-received note to Jill, with Mar and David copied, confirming the request was added to the CM-CoE active evaluation queue.

items.label: Make the Amazon Connect delay summary decision-ready
items.detail: Keep the executive summary and supporting artifact focused on whether they answer Jon’s ask on Amazon Connect and Agentforce Voice delays. Flag any remaining owner questions, missing timeline context, or gaps for Danny and Jeff.

items.label: Close the EOW programmatic update path
items.detail: Use the EOW check-in output to pin down which updates need leadership attention, especially anything tied to Amazon Connect, intake routing, or portfolio review.

items.label: Land the EPMO intake and BA-request process
items.detail: Keep the Anu, Janet, and Jorge process conversation moving toward a clear path for department and enterprise BA requests, including whether the bi-weekly IT prioritization touchpoint becomes the cadence for priority shifts.

items.label: Bring clear blockers to SMS Automation status
items.detail: The SMS Automation path still needs clean next steps on blockers, owners, risks, and escalations. Laura’s latest email also keeps the marketing number setup, short-code process, and opt-out list source questions in the same workstream.

items.label: Check the dashboard PR #7 preview only if it affects visibility
items.detail: Treat the pmdashboard PR #7 preview as a quick verification item if Jean needs confidence that the Planner task queue snapshot is visible before it merges.
